title: Wrong Corner Trap
main_student_action: Move to a corner, commit to a word-part choice, and defend it.
what_it_is: A movement-based routine where students choose a response location based on which prefix, suffix, root, or mixed word-part option makes sense.
why_it_stays_in_bank: It runs cold, scales easily, and keeps explanation pressure attached to real literacy thinking instead of speed alone.
non_negotiable_game_rule: Students must be able to defend their corner using real-word or meaning logic, not only speed or guessing.
teacher_script: Move when you know your answer, then be ready to defend it.
Some rounds have one correct answer, some have more than one, and some are traps with none.
student_job: Choose a corner, justify the choice, and stay alert for trap rounds.

versions.label: movement core
versions.purpose: Fast whole-class engagement with visible commitment.

versions.label: whiteboard fallback
versions.purpose: Keep the same logic when movement is not practical.

required_literacy_protections: every reveal must return to word logic
traps must be plausible, not random
teacher names why an answer is real, fake, or arguable
easier_version: keep one clearly unrelated option visible
harder_version: require a second example before awarding full points
good_teacher_language: Pick your corner and be ready to prove it.
Do not follow the crowd unless you can explain the move.
extension_questions: What clue helped you know the word was real or not real?
exit_slip_options: Use one winning word part in a real word.
Name one trap and explain why it failed.
